content: How to debug a kernel module with gdb server and rust-analyzer in VSCode? What do I need to setup?

There are no official ways of running a kernel debugger with Linux. The options I'm aware of are:

  • Run in qemu and use the qemu gdbserver. This will behave badly when the thread you are debugging gets context switched away as qemu is not aware of this. It presents each cpu core as a thread to gdb independent of which kernel task is running on said core. https://www.kernel.org/doc/html/latest/dev-tools/gdb-kernel-debugging.html
  • Use drgn inside the VM. Drgn is read-only, so you can't eg set breakpoints. And even if you could, given that it runs in userspace, you can easily deadlock yourself. https://developers.facebook.com/blog/post/2021/12/09/drgn-how-linux-kernel-team-meta-debugs-kernel-scale/
  • Something like kdb or kgdb. Not sure if that still works with the latest kernel though. Also generally needs two machines (or running the kernel to debug in a VM). https://landley.net/kdocs/Documentation/DocBook/xhtml-nochunks/kgdb.html#CompilingAKernel
Let's say I choose one of these options, what are the steps I need to follow to debug the rust code? Right now I use virtme-ng which uses qemu behind the scenes afaik.
Qemu basically just needs the -s -S flags then you can attach a debugger, then you can gdb "{{source_dir}}/vmlinux" -ex 'target remote localhost:1234'
I'm sure virtime exposes that somehow but I'm not familiar with it
Oh, looks pretty easy. Their docs have a "Attach a gdb session to a running instance started with --debug" section https://github.com/arighi/virtme-ng

I have done this part. But I want to reach software level debugging using the VSCode debugger. I have created launch.json to run all these commands. But it works only for c/c++ not rust. Maybe I somehow need to find a way to load the rust debug symbols I guess.
https://github.com/tur11ng/linux-kernel-devcontainer. This is my setup. Anyways I was wondering if there is any kind of guide, otherwise I will have to do my research.
What exactly is happening or not happening? The debug symbols are all the same, but I wonder if the distinction is compiled-in works vs. plugin/module works, rather than c works but rust doesn't
